title: sendTransaction used instead of sendAndConfirmTransaction — transaction may silently drop

params.passDetail: Handler calls sendAndConfirmTransaction, which waits for the cluster to confirm the transaction before returning and propagates any on-chain error as a thrown exception.
params.failDetail: Handler calls connection.sendTransaction() but not sendAndConfirmTransaction(). sendTransaction() submits the transaction and returns a signature immediately, with no confirmation that it landed. If the transaction is dropped (congestion, validator restart, blockhash expiry) the code continues as if it succeeded — tokens are not moved but the caller assumes they were. Use sendAndConfirmTransaction(connection, tx, signers) to block until confirmation.
params.absentDetail: Handler is in the send/transfer scope but calls neither sendTransaction nor sendAndConfirmTransaction; this rule does not apply here.

provenance.note: connection.sendTransaction() is a legacy fire-and-forget call. Real-world instances: AI-generated payout scripts that call sendTransaction() and immediately credit the user without waiting for confirmation. The official Solana docs and numerous postmortems recommend sendAndConfirmTransaction() for any value-bearing path.
